title: Synch rate and pilot injuries
wikitext:
[]: Synch rate and pilot injuries             Quote Post by HENRCAST » Fri Oct 10, 2008 9:11 pm There's this one thing that's really bugging me after I watched EoE: It is fairly clear that because of the synch system the pilots feel pain when their Evas sustain damage. There are a number of examples in this case: in Ep.18 Eva 00's arm is severed, and Rei is clearly in pain and in Ep.19 both of Eva 02's arms are severed and Asuka is clearly in pain as well. But in EoE Asuka is clearly wounded because her Eva took damage. This is clearly seen when her eye starts to bleed rapidly and when her arm is split in half. But when the MP Evas "eat" 02's torso, Asuka is seen in pain, but her torso isn't torn apart. Also, in the manga, after 02's arms are taken out by that turtle looking Angel (Ep.19 in the anime), Asuka rushes at him, and just before Unit 02 got decaptated, the nerval connections betwenn her and the Eva are cut. After that, she seems surprised by the fact that her head is still there, making us believe that if the nerval connections hadn't been she would have died. So as it seems, sometimes the pilots only feel pain, and sometimes they're injured themselves. But why is that? Why does Asuka only feels pain when Unit 02's arms are severed and in EoE she is actually hurt?

[]:             Quote Post by child of Lilith » Fri Oct 10, 2008 11:18 pm It probably has a lot to do with how high the synch rate is. When Unit 2’s arms were cut off her synch rate was probably nowhere near the level it was at when she later fought the Harpies. As for Asuka’s stomach not being torn when the Harpies were attacking Unit 2, She had just run out of power and her synch rate was most likely very low. It wasn’t until she started do go into berserker mode that the damage to Unit 2 became visible on her. This would also explain her eye getting pierced by the lance. After all the lance hit just before she ran out of power.

[]:             Quote Post by CorporalChaos » Sat Oct 11, 2008 12:18 am Aside from the high sync ratios Asuka has with Unit-02, I also recall the Lance of Longinus's use having something to do with the injuries. It might be an impression I recieved from fanfiction or something. I'm not really sure. So, in lieu of evidence to the contrary, I just say a high sync ratio. Unless someone else would like to step up with more evidence of it being the Lance as well.

[]:             Quote Post by NemZ » Sat Oct 11, 2008 2:48 am I really want to say it's all in her head, as are other instances of pilots feeling eva injuries even if they are shown affecting them as well, but that argument gets really ugly quickly when one can't tell if things in the show actually happen or not.

[]:             Quote Post by Dartz » Sat Oct 11, 2008 6:56 am The injuries to Asuka's eye and arm were caused by attacks from the false Lance of Longinus's. You see it happen. We dont see any injuries on Asuka when the Eva-series pull Unit 02 apart, because it's just a 'standard' sort of attack (But we don't see inside the plugsuit either mind), a damaging attack on the Eva itself. Asuka is still able to make the remains of Unit 02 go berserker . But the lance attack is different somehow, so when it hits Unit 02's arm, the exact same damage is caused to Asuka herself. And of course, when another eight lances follow them in... it's no going to be very conductive towards the pilot's survival. So anyway, it seems that Lances of longinus have some sort of power that allows them to penetrate AT-fields, and this power also allows them to directly harm the pilot in a way that mirrors the damage to the Eva. It's a weapon effect,

[]:             Quote Post by HENRCAST » Sat Oct 11, 2008 9:13 am Dartz wrote: The injuries to Asuka's eye and arm were caused by attacks from the false Lance of Longinus's. You see it happen. We dont see any injuries on Asuka when the Eva-series pull Unit 02 apart, because it's just a 'standard' sort of attack (But we don't see inside the plugsuit either mind), a damaging attack on the Eva itself. Asuka is still able to make the remains of Unit 02 go berserker . But the lance attack is different somehow, so when it hits Unit 02's arm, the exact same damage is caused to Asuka herself. And of course, when another eight lances follow them in... it's no going to be very conductive towards the pilot's survival.

[]:             Quote Post by rberlin » Sat Oct 11, 2008 10:04 am I always assumed it was the sync ratio. Near 100% you sustained all the damage Eva does. Above 100% you get sucked into the core. Maybe I'm wrong, but it seems that the better a pilot, the more damage they receive.

[]:             Quote Post by Dartz » Sat Oct 11, 2008 10:32 am HENRCAST wrote: That may be it. So if that's the case, then Asuka could have died even if not a single false LoL hit the entry plug, because she gets injured when the Lances hit Unit 02. But what about the manga? I know it is considered non-canon, but as it seems, Asuka's head should have fallen off after Unit 02 got decaptated, but since they had cut out the nerval connection, it didn't. That happens in the anime too, but Asuka doesn't say "Is my head still here?". As far as I can remember, the turtle looking angel didn't have a false LoL.

[]:             Quote Post by V » Sat Oct 11, 2008 10:48 am the answer is simple: at incredibly high synch ratios (such as what Asuka achieved in EoE, her best ever) you physically suffer sympathetic damage of everything that happens to the Eva. At normal levels it just FEELS like that but at higher levels it actually happens.

[]:             Quote Post by Dartz » Sat Oct 11, 2008 10:51 am V wrote: the answer is simple: at incredibly high synch ratios (such as what Asuka achieved in EoE, her best ever) you physically suffer sympathetic damage of everything that happens to the Eva. At normal levels it just FEELS like that but at higher levels it actually happens.

[]:             Quote Post by UrsusArctos » Sat Oct 11, 2008 10:57 am Dartz wrote: Turtle Angel? Which one looked like a turtle?

[]:             Quote Post by Iron Rooster » Sat Oct 11, 2008 11:10 am Unit 02 was out of power when the MP's were ripping her apart. But she still felt like (or was) she was being disemboweled. I can't recall, but was there a blood stain near where her guts would be in her plug suit when she was writhing and grabbing her abdomen? I guess during that whole fiasco, she had an abnormal synch ratio which explains why she still felt the injuries AFTER the power went out.

[]:             Quote Post by Silent Lightning » Sat Oct 11, 2008 2:10 pm This whole Zeruel=turtle thing is rather insulting to Zeruel, I think... In any case, it does seem that the LnY inflicts more bodily damage to the pilot that external wounds to the Eva otherwise would. Why that's the case I have no idea...probably time to ask the FAR. I suppose an explanation can be scrounged out of the AT Field strengths with regards to the pilot and the Field-piercing nature of the LnY...? I dunno, get someone other than me to do it.

[]:             Quote Post by AyrYntake » Sat Oct 11, 2008 4:51 pm Well, if you consider synchronization as an overlap of the pilot's and the Eva Unit's AT Fields (an overlapping of the soul), then the damage caused to the Evangelion body by an AT Field-piercing spear would certainly echo in the pilot's body, not only neurally but bodily due to the soul's involvement in it. The problem with this is that at this point, quantum magic becomes involved with all these things.

[]:             Quote Post by V » Sat Oct 11, 2008 5:24 pm Dartz wrote: But if that was the case, then shouldnt Asuka have literally been ripped apart by the Eva-series, when she was driving Unit 01 berserk. I didnt see any intestines bursting torugh her plugsuit, or arms/eyes head being torn apart. She shure as hell felt it alright.... but if she'd suffered those sort of unjuries to her person, she would probably have died immediately. If you rewatch the scene is planly obvious that the only physical damage comes from the lances, everything else is just psychosomatic....

[]:             Quote Post by Dartz » Sat Oct 11, 2008 5:51 pm If her guts were torn to pieces and her lungs ripped out and her body inside her plugsuit turned to so much mincemeat, chances are she wouldnt've been able to do much more that gurgle and die...

[]:             Quote Post by Axell » Sat Oct 11, 2008 6:04 pm I dont think it had anything to do with eva2 being out of power. Here are my thoughts: Sustaining battle damage due to high sync ratios is actually all phycological pain made physical. Let me explain..... I've heard that if you convince someone beyond all doubt that your about to torture them by burning them with a hot metal rod that touching them with a room temperature rod can actually result in the brain believing that the skin is being burned and will actually cause some form of scarring or burning. Ritsuko says that synchronization with an eva is all mental, so if the above is actually true, that may be what actually happens to the pilots. As sync ratios get higher, the pain becomes more and more real until damage is actually sustained. This could answer what was happening to Asuka before she was killed. it would be physically impossible (at least in the real world) for her guts to be lifted up slowly from her chest, they would just suffer internal damage and or separate from each other. I'll see if i can find an article about the brain body damage thing to back my theory up

[]:             Quote Post by Iron Rooster » Sat Oct 11, 2008 7:23 pm Is it like how in The Matrix when somebody sustains injuries in it, their brain makes it real? That's sounds like the general idea. And does anyone have a screenshot of her writing in pain? I don't know if her abdomen was bleeding or not.

[]:             Quote Post by Axell » Sat Oct 11, 2008 10:57 pm thats actually exactly what i mean.

[]:             Quote Post by wintermute » Sun Oct 12, 2008 3:04 am I certainly don't understand the mechanics of it but I agree that a pilot's psychic bond with their Eva would go beyond motor control to also experiencing (sharing?) physical sensations, even the most extreme and unpleasant ones, during the course of a battle. When taken far enough, it would probably push a body into various states of shock, which would probably account for the various "wounds" the pilots receive throughout the series. Are they authentic injuries or simply "mere" psychic traumas that would feel just as real? I don't know either. Axell wrote: I've heard that if you convince someone beyond all doubt that your about to torture them by burning them with a hot metal rod that touching them with a room temperature rod can actually result in the brain believing that the skin is being burned and will actually cause some form of scarring or burning.

There are a number of examples in this case: in Ep.18 Eva 00's arm is severed, and Rei is clearly in pain and in Ep.19 both of Eva 02's arms are severed and A
